mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, supply-side constraints force firms to cut capital expenditures and delay hiring, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below the 12-month average are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observations during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, inflation expectations become unanchored and force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above the 220 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while real personal consumption expenditures decelerates indicates corporate profit margin compression from rising input costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric yields a Rank IC below 0.03 for predicting negative 20-day returns over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a monthly decline in industrial production confirms that policy is effectively slowing demand, which lowers recession probability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of cases.
